deviation_assessment.deviation: Dev disabled free-text scan (\b(complete|passed|done|verified|confirmed|exists)\b) for spawn-request files. Structural field checks (status/verdict/exists) are retained for spawn-requests per EDGE-6.
deviation_assessment.reasoning: Spec §2 explicitly states 'Rule 1 does NOT fire on spawn-request files (Rule 2 territory).' EDGE-6 is a defined exception that requires BLOCK when a spawn-request contains structural terminal-state fields (status/verdict/exists) with no evidence. Dev reconciles these correctly: apply structural checks to spawn-requests (satisfies EDGE-6), skip free-text (consistent with §2's exclusion and the legitimate use of terminal words in prompt text). No spec case requires free-text blocking on spawn-requests — R1-BLOCK-4 ('done' in summary) uses a sage-result path, not a spawn-request path. The gap scenario (free-text terminal word in a non-prompt spawn-request field) is not defined as a BLOCK in the spec. Deviation is spec-compliant.
deviation_assessment.gap_analysis: A spawn-request file with {'summary':'Migration is done'} (no status/verdict/exists fields) would ALLOW. This is correct per spec: §2 scopes Rule 1 away from spawn-requests as a category, and the spec does not define this as a BLOCK case. No hole.
